descriptor: Physical Causal Evidence and Verified Intervention Research
rationale: Inbar is the Hebrew word for amber and has personal meaning to Daniel Wahnich. The name is short, durable, easy to spell, and independent of a narrow implementation. The mission binds physical evidence, intervention, and settled outcomes into causal claims that can be preserved and inspected.

portfolio_relationship.Inbar: turns physical evidence and verified intervention into defensible causal claims

legacy_boundary.name: Fieldtrue
legacy_boundary.status: retained only as the historical package, protocol, schema, and evidence namespace
legacy_boundary.rule: Never rewrite signed history or frozen identifiers for an identity migration.

current_collision_audit.adjacent_sector_finding: No major exact-name AI model, robotics platform, or aerospace research mission collision was found in the preliminary screen.
known_uses: INBAR is the International Bamboo and Rattan Organization.
INBAR Software is used in Israeli traffic-signal planning.
Inbar Space is an aerospace consulting business.
inbar.es is a restaurant inventory platform.

pre_publication_gate: open-source license and third-party rights review
symbol_direction: A precise amber inclusion intersects a measured causal path and settles against a fixed datum. The geometry should communicate preserved evidence, intervention, and physical resolution without a literal shield.
symbol_constraints: legible as favicon, telemetry glyph, technical-document mark, and spacecraft stencil
